Off Calendar Jun-07-2023 Continued To Aug-23-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-31-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Thomas Bizzaro, Esq. 631-663-0971 Law Offices for Plaintiff.
David Fuad, Esq 213-612-2369 Orrick Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP for Defendant.
Court reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR#13293, 415-274-9977, sheilapham28@gmail.com

RULING - A R G U E D. Mr. Fuad contests the tentative ruling and argues claims 3 and 4; breach of contract and breach of implied covenant. Mr. Bizzaro responds and request the court to adhere to the tentative ruling. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative rule as follows:

DEFENDANT ASCELLA BIOSYSTEMS, INC., DEEPAK BOGGAVARAPU'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT

Defendants' demurrer to the complaint is sustained in part and overruled in part.

1: Fraud. While the complaint provides a little detail, it fails to plead fraud with the required specificity - "how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered." (Stansfield v. Starkey (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 59, 73.) After that is pled, the court may be able to reach the parties' arguments. Demurrer sustained with leave to amend.

2: Negligent Misrepresentation. The same alleged representations are at issue here (Cmplt. 7:6-11), so the ruling is the same - demurrer sustained with leave to amend.

3: Breach of Contract. Unlike fraud, this claim need not be pled with particularity. The complaint adequately pleads the elements of contract breach, and if, as here, the contract is attached to the complaint, plaintiff's interpretation of it must be accepted as correct on a general demurrer. (Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239.) Demurrer overruled.

4: Breach of Implied Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing. Plaintiffs assert that, under their interpretation of the contract, the implied covenant claim adds no terms. Again, plaintiffs' interpretation must be accepted at the pleading stage. (Aragon-Haas, 231 Cal.App.3d at 239.) Demurrer overruled.

5: Breach of Fiduciary Duty. A company officer or promotor may be a fiduciary to an investor, as plaintiffs plead here. (Cleveland v. Johnson (2012) 209 Cal.App.4th 1315, 1338-39.) Whether the business judgment rule applies is for factual development. Demurrer overruled.

6: Conversion. The complaint adequately pleads a claim for conversion. Demurrer overruled.

7: Accounting. The demurrer relies on arguments already addressed; it is overruled.

Mr. Bizzaro to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B, Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR#13293, 415-274-9977, sheilapham28@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
